description: Skilled in woodworking, construction, and toolmaking
materialRequirements: Wood and stone for workshop structures

productions.description: Produces tools and crafted items daily

interdependenceDescription: Adds levels to all production villagers through better tools
randomTalk: Wood is honest. It tells you exactly where it wants to split. Usually through your thumbnail.
People think carpentry is simple. 'Just nail bits of wood together,' they say. Then their house falls down and suddenly I'm the expert.
Every plank was a tree once. Every tree was a seed. Every seed was a... look, I think about this too much.
The grain of this wood is magnificent. You don't care, do you? Nobody ever cares about the grain.
I've built more workbenches than I can count. Each one slightly better than the last. It's like a metaphor for something.
Splinters are just the wood's way of coming home with you.
They asked me to build a round table. Do you have any idea how hard round is? Everything WANTS to be square.
A good joint needs no nails. A great joint needs no glue. A perfect joint needs a Carpenter who hasn't slept in three days.
I could build you a chair that lasts a thousand years. Or I could build you one that's comfortable. Pick one.
Sometimes I look at a tree and see a beautiful home. The tree looks at me and sees a maniac with an axe. Perspective.
I'm not saying I'm the backbone of this village, but have you tried sitting down without furniture?
Yes, I can hear the walls creaking. No, it's not structural. Probably.
Look, I just really like walnut.
randomGreets: Come in! Mind the sawdust--it's everywhere.
Ah, hello! I was just having a word with this oak plank. Don't ask.
Welcome to the workshop! Everything you see, I built. Including the floor you're standing on.
Need something built? Knocked down? Artfully rearranged?
Well met! You're looking a bit... unvarnished today.
Pull up a chair! I made it myself. Obviously.
randomGoodbye: Off you go. These shelves won't build themselves. Believe me, I've asked.
Safe travels! Be wary of blacksmiths and anyone named 'Olaf'
Take care! And if you find any good timber out there, you know where I am.
May your roof never leak. Unless you want it to!
